evals.prompt: I just cloned an unfamiliar Android repository. Find the smallest real Android project root and tell me the next standard command you would run without kicking off a full build.
evals.expected_output: The answer identifies one concrete Android project root, explains why it is the right root, and proposes a small read-only wrapper inspection command from that root.
assertions: The answer identifies one exact Android project root
The answer prefers file inspection before broad Gradle inspection
The answer recommends a small next command rather than a full build or tasks --all

evals.prompt: This Android app still uses jcenter(), apply plugin, and an old wrapper. Decide whether it needs modernization guidance and outline the first safe upgrade step.
evals.expected_output: The answer states that modernization guidance applies, names concrete legacy signals, and starts with source-of-truth and compatibility inspection rather than an unsafe version jump.
assertions: The answer identifies modernization triggers from the prompt or inspected build files
The answer treats compatibility as a Gradle, AGP, Kotlin, and JDK question rather than a single version bump
The answer proposes a first safe inspection step before editing
The answer avoids prescribing arbitrary version numbers without context

evals.prompt: An Android screen looks wrong on device. Give me the smallest adb-driven UI triage plan that keeps token usage down and only reads hierarchy XML or logcat if the screenshot is not enough.
evals.expected_output: The answer makes a reduced screenshot the default, explains when hierarchy XML is actually needed, and keeps diagnostics bounded.
assertions: The plan starts with a screenshot capture and reduced copy
The plan treats hierarchy XML as conditional rather than mandatory
The plan avoids reading the full XML dump by default
The plan keeps logcat bounded instead of streaming indefinitely
